target.title: Harden Release CLI manual dispatch
target.body:
Fixes #1153.

## Summary

- make manual `Release CLI` dispatches artifact-only, even when `tag` is provided
- keep GitHub release asset uploads limited to `release` events
- keep the Homebrew tap update limited to `release` events
- clarify that the manual `tag` input packages artifacts but does not publish them

## Why

The current workflow lets a manual dispatch build from the selected workflow ref while using `inputs.tag` as `RELEASE_TAG`. Because the upload step runs when `inputs.tag != ''` and uses `gh release upload "$TAG" ... --clobber`, a manual run can publish assets for a release tag from a different checkout ref.

ClawSweeper validated #1153 as source-repro, P1, impact:security, and fix-shape-clear. It also noted that a live repro against the real repo should not be run because it would mutate release assets.

This draft chooses the narrowest policy fix ClawSweeper suggested: manual runs build workflow artifacts only; publishing remains tied to release events.

## Proof

Issue proof:

- #1153 has ClawSweeper `clawsweeper:source-repro`, `P1`, `impact:security`, `issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster`, and `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`.
- ClawSweeper's review says source inspection gives a high-confidence path: manual dispatch supplies `inputs.tag`, checkout remains on the workflow ref, and upload publishes to that tag with `--clobber`.

Patch proof:

```text
$ ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/release-cli.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'
yaml ok

$ rg -n "if: github.event_name == 'release'$|if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'$" .github/workflows/release-cli.yml
214:        if: github.event_name == 'release'
226:        if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
237:    if: github.event_name == 'release'

$ rg -n "inputs\\.tag != ''|github\\.event_name != 'release' && inputs\\.tag == ''" .github/workflows/release-cli.yml
# no matches

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

Live fork proof:

I manually dispatched the patched `Release CLI` workflow on my fork with a harmless proof tag:

```text
$ gh workflow run release-cli.yml --repo jskoiz/CodexBar --ref fix/release-cli-tag-provenance -f tag=v-proof-1154
https://github.com/jskoiz/CodexBar/actions/runs/26432040743
```

Run result:

- event: `workflow_dispatch`
- branch: `fix/release-cli-tag-provenance`
- commit: `45e2ad1dd5b16036e6b088de49970cb00a291c91`
- conclusion: `success`
- `update-homebrew-tap`: `skipped`

Relevant job-step results from the run:

```text
macos-x86_64: Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
linux-x64:    Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
linux-arm64:  Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
macos-arm64:  Upload release assets = skipped; Upload workflow artifact (manual runs) = success
```

Artifacts were uploaded for all four targets:

```text
codexbar-cli-macos-x86_64
codexbar-cli-linux-x64
codexbar-cli-linux-arm64
codexbar-cli-macos-arm64
```

One downloaded artifact confirms the manual tag was packaged as an artifact rather than published as a release asset:

```text
/tmp/codexbar-proof-artifacts/CodexBarCLI-v-proof-1154-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
/tmp/codexbar-proof-artifacts/CodexBarCLI-v-proof-1154-linux-x86_64.tar.gz.sha256
```

The commit uses `[skip ci]` to avoid implicit macOS CI on push/PR creation; the run above was an explicit fork workflow dispatch for this proof. If maintainers prefer preserving a manual repair-publish path, the alternate shape is to check out the requested tag, verify `HEAD` matches that tag before upload, and avoid `--clobber` by default.
